title: fix: drop retry loop in image backend dispatch
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Remove 3-attempt retry loop in `fetchFromLeastBusyServer`
- Single fetch; throw `HttpError` on non-OK with status, body, and upstreamUrl
- Caller already documents "Single attempt - no retry logic"
- Unblocks #10965: 4xx classification now sees the structured `HttpError`

gist.headline: One and done
gist.blurb: We've streamlined the image backend dispatch by removing a redundant retry loop. Now it makes a single attempt and throws a clean error, paving the way for better error handling.
gist.summary: Removed the 3-attempt retry loop in fetchFromLeastBusyServer to rely on a single fetch attempt that throws a structured HttpError on failure.
gist.impact: This internal cleanup ensures upstream errors are passed through correctly, unblocking improved 4xx error classification.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot looking at a single, clean, straight wire or code block, having removed a tangled loop. Represents the removal of the retry loop for a single, clean attempt.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and a chunky monitor-headed robot inspecting a single glowing lime green (#ecf874) code block on a retro terminal, having just untangled a messy loop of wires. Soft pastel lavender and cream gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, chunky sprites, emotionally warm.
